title: CLAUDE.md
text: fastRhockey R package accessing hockey data NHL PWHL via public web APIs. package also contains deprecated functions defunct PHF league. Part SportsDataverse family. R >= 4.0.0 | License: MIT | testthat edition 3 | Version: 1.0.0

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Install dependencies devtools::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE)  # Build documentation (roxygen2) devtools::document()  # Run full R CMD check devtools::check()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-nhl_standings.R")  # Build and install locally devtools::install()  # Build pkgdown site pkgdown::build_site()

title: CLAUDE.md
code: R/                          # Source code (one function per file)   utils.R                   # Shared helpers: check_status, most_recent_*_season, messaging   zzz.R                     # .onLoad() — xG model download/caching lifecycle   fastRhockey-package.R     # Package-level roxygen2 documentation, lifecycle import   helpers_nhl.R             # helper_nhl_calculate_xg(), helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data()   pwhl_helpers.R            # .pwhl_api(), .pwhl_modulekit_url(), .pwhl_gc_url() — PWHL JSONP helpers   nhl_*.R                   # NHL functions (api-web.nhle.com + api.nhle.com/stats)   phf_*.R                   # PHF functions (deprecated — league defunct)   pwhl_*.R                  # PWHL functions (lscluster.hockeytech.com)  tests/testthat/             # Unit tests (testthat edition 3) man/                        # Auto-generated roxygen2 documentation data/                       # Included datasets (.rda) data-raw/                   # Scripts for generating package data (*.md files untracked) vignettes/                  # Vignettes for pkgdown site

title: CLAUDE.md
text: NHL Web API returns clean JSON parsed jsonlite::fromJSON() NHL Stats API uses Cayenne filter expressions query params HockeyTech returns JSONP Angular callbacks must regex-stripped parsing PWHL internal helpers pwhl_helpers.R: .pwhl_api() strips JSONP, .pwhl_modulekit_url() / .pwhl_gc_url() build URLs

title: CLAUDE.md
text: data-returning functions wrap output fastRhockey_data via make_fastRhockey_data(). Tests check: expect_s3_class(x, "fastRhockey_data").

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Three XGBoost models (5v5, special teams, penalty shots) downloaded .onLoad() sportsdataverse/fastRhockey-nhl-data/main/models/, cached tools::R_user_dir("fastRhockey", "cache"). Requires xgboost suggested package. Models stored .xg_env (package-level environment zzz.R) accessed via .xg_env$xg_model_5v5 etc. helpers_nhl.R.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package uses lifecycle formal deprecation PHF functions: - PHF functions (21 functions): Deprecated v1.0.0 — league ceased operations - deprecated functions emit lifecycle::deprecate_stop() raise errors - Tests deprecated functions check lifecycle_error_deprecated class

title: CLAUDE.md
text: One exported function per file; filename matches function name httr::RETRY("GET", ...) HTTP requests janitor::clean_names() API responses tryCatch message() errors — functions return NULL failure, errors glue::glue() URL string interpolation Internal helpers prefixed . (e.g., .parse_game_rosters(), .pwhl_api()) globalVariables() utils.R suppress R CMD check NSE notes Types: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, build, ci, chore Scopes: nhl, phf, pwhl, xg, pkgdown, ci, loader

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Exported: league_entity_action() (e.g., nhl_teams_roster(), pwhl_schedule()) Internal: .snake_case() leading dot (e.g., .parse_game_rosters(), .pwhl_api()) Tests: test-function_name.R Parameters: snake_case (e.g., game_id, team_abbr, season) Datasets: snake_case .rda files

title: CLAUDE.md
text: NHL Web API / Stats API: "20242025" (concatenated years) most_recent_nhl_season() returns end year numeric (e.g., 2025) most_recent_nhl_season_api_param() returns "20242025" format PWHL: numeric year (e.g., 2025) most_recent_pwhl_season() returns concluding year numeric (e.g., 2025) pwhl_season_id() maps (season, game_type) → HockeyTech season_id .pwhl_resolve_season_id(season, game_type) internal convenience wrapper

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Framework: testthat edition 3 Run : devtools::test() Environment: Set NOT_CRAN=true run full test suite locally Skip helpers: tests/testthat/helper-skip.R provides skip_nhl_test(), skip_phf_test(), skip_pwhl_test() controlled env vars RUN_NHL_TESTS, RUN_PHF_TESTS, RUN_PWHL_TESTS (NHL/PWHL default true, PHF false). CI workflow uses variable names. Pattern: skip_on_cran() + skip_*_test(), check data.frame + fastRhockey_data classes, validate nrow(x) > 0, check expected column names xG tests: additionally require skip_if_not_installed("xgboost") Deprecated functions: wrap calls suppressWarnings() tests Reproducibility: use specific game IDs/dates (e.g., game_id = 2023020001, PWHL game_id = 27)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Never include AI tools (Claude, Copilot, ChatGPT, etc.) co-author author commit messages. Commits attributed solely human contributors made directed changes.

text: One exported function per file Filename matches function name (e.g., nhl_standings.R contains nhl_standings()) Internal helper functions prefixed . (e.g., .parse_response())

title: Contributing to fastRhockey
text: Use %>% (magrittr pipe) chaining Use janitor::clean_names() API responses Use httr::RETRY("GET", ...) HTTP requests (built-retry) Use glue::glue() URL string interpolation Maximum line length: strict limit, keep readable

title: Contributing to fastRhockey
text: API-calling functions must follow pattern: Functions return NULL failure – never throw errors user.
code: tryCatch(     expr = {         res <- httr::RETRY("GET", url)         check_status(res)         # ... parse and return     },     error = function(e) {         message(glue::glue("{Sys.time()}: Error in func_name: {e$message}"))         return(NULL)     } )

code: test_that("LEAGUE - Descriptive test name", {     skip_on_cran()     x <- function_name(param = value)      expect_s3_class(x, "data.frame")     expect_s3_class(x, "fastRhockey_data")     expect_true(nrow(x) > 0)      expected_cols <- c("col1", "col2")     for (col in expected_cols) {         expect_true(col %in% names(x), info = paste("Missing column:", col))     } })

title: Contributing to fastRhockey
text: Tests package make live API calls skipped CRAN via skip_on_cran(). run full test suite locally: NOT_CRAN=true: Set environment variable skip_on_cran() skip tests. can set .Renviron file inline: League-specific test toggles (via tests/testthat/helper-skip.R): Set running tests: CI, set .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml : NHL_TESTS=1, PWHL_TESTS=1, PHF_TESTS=0. GITHUB_PAT: loader functions access data GitHub repositories sportsdataverse org. Set GitHub personal access token avoid rate limits: Reproducible test data: Use specific, known game IDs, dates, seasons tests (e.g., game_id = 2023020001, season = 2024) rather most_recent_*_season() helpers, test expectations remain stable across seasons.
code: Sys.setenv(NOT_CRAN = "true") devtools::test() Sys.setenv(NOT_CRAN = "true", RUN_NHL_TESTS = "true", RUN_PWHL_TESTS = "true") devtools::test() # In ~/.Renviron GITHUB_PAT=ghp_your_token_here

text: function needs deprecated (e.g., API retired function superseded better alternative), use lifecycle package:

title: Contributing to fastRhockey
code: #' @description #' `r lifecycle::badge("deprecated")` #' #' This function is deprecated. Use [new_function()] instead. my_old_function <- function(param) {     lifecycle::deprecate_warn(         when = "1.0.0",         what = "my_old_function()",         with = "my_new_function()"     )     # Delegate to the new function     my_new_function(param) }

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: can install CRAN version fastRhockey : can install development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("fastRhockey") if (!requireNamespace('pak', quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages('pak') } pak::pak("sportsdataverse/fastRhockey")

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: PWHL (Professional Women’s Hockey League) launched January 2024 six teams: Boston, Minnesota, Montreal, New York, Ottawa, Toronto. fastRhockey provides full access PWHL schedules, standings, play--play, player box scores, team rosters, stat leaders.

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
code: library(dplyr)  pwhl_teams()

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: Let’s pull 2024 PWHL season schedule: schedule returns game IDs, dates, teams, scores, venue information every game season.
code: schedule <- pwhl_schedule(season = 2024)  schedule %>%   dplyr::filter(game_status == "Final") %>%   dplyr::select(game_id, game_date, home_team, away_team,                 home_score, away_score, winner, venue) %>%   head(10)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
code: pwhl_standings(season = 2024) %>%   dplyr::select(team_rank, team, games_played, points,                 wins, losses, goals_for, goals_against)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: Pick game schedule pull individual player stats. pwhl_player_box() returns list two data frames: skaters goalies.
code: box <- pwhl_player_box(game_id = 27)  # Skater stats box[[1]] %>%   dplyr::select(first_name, last_name, position, team_id,                 goals, assists, points, shots, toi) %>%   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(points)) %>%   head(10)  # Goalie stats box[[2]] %>%   dplyr::select(first_name, last_name, team_id,                 saves, goals_against, shots_against, toi)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: real power fastRhockey play--play data. pwhl_pbp() returns detailed event-level data including shot locations, faceoff results, penalties, goals assist information, plus/minus player tracking. play--play data includes 90+ columns event details, player information, coordinates (raw transformed), game context.
code: pbp <- pwhl_pbp(game_id = 27)  pbp %>%   dplyr::select(event, team_id, period_of_game, time_of_period,                 player_name_first, player_name_last,                 x_coord, y_coord) %>%   head(15)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: Pull league-wide goalie skater stats:
code: # Goalie stats pwhl_stats(position = "goalie", season = 2024) %>%   dplyr::select(player_name, team, games_played,                 goals_against_avg, save_percentage, shutouts) %>%   head(10) # Skater stats (all teams) pwhl_stats(position = "skater", season = 2024) %>%   dplyr::select(player_name, team, games_played,                 goals, assists, points) %>%   head(10)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: fastRhockey also provides extensive access NHL data via api-web.nhle.com api.nhle.com/stats APIs.

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
code: nhl_schedule(season = "20242025") %>%   head(10)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
code: nhl_standings(season = "20242025") %>%   head(10)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: nhl_game_feed() function returns detailed game data including play--play, rosters, game information. nhl_game_pbp() provides play--play shift data integrated.
code: feed <- nhl_game_feed(game_id = 2024020001)  dplyr::glimpse(feed)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
code: # Skater season stats nhl_stats_skaters(season = "20242025") %>%   head(10)

code: # Get 2024 draft picks (round 1) nhl_draft_year(year = 2024, round = 1)

code: # Full season of play-by-play pbp <- load_nhl_pbp(seasons = 2024)  # Full season schedule sched <- load_nhl_schedule(seasons = 2024)  # Full season rosters rosters <- load_nhl_rosters(seasons = 2024)

text: Premier Hockey Federation (PHF), formerly National Women’s Hockey League (NWHL), ceased operations prior formation PWHL. phf_* functions deprecated v1.0.0 raise errors called. Use pwhl_* functions instead women’s professional hockey data.

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: fastRhockey R package accessing hockey data NHL (National Hockey League) PWHL (Professional Women’s Hockey League). wraps public web APIs (NHL API, HockeyTech) provide structured tibbles play--play, schedule, standings, roster, draft, player stats, team data. package also ships integrated expected goals (xG) models trained via XGBoost cached locally. package includes deprecated functions defunct PHF (Premier Hockey Federation) league. emit lifecycle warnings removed future release. Part SportsDataverse family R/Python packages sports analytics. Version: 1.0.0 R version: >= 4.0.0 License: MIT Documentation site: https://fastRhockey.sportsdataverse.org/ GitHub: https://github.com/sportsdataverse/fastRhockey

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
code: # Install all dependencies (dev + suggests) install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE)  # Run full R CMD check devtools::check()  # Build documentation (roxygen2) devtools::document()  # Run tests devtools::test() # or testthat::test_local()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-nhl_standings.R")  # Build and install locally devtools::install()  # Build pkgdown site pkgdown::build_site()

(e.g., .parse_game_rosters(), .build_pbp()) globalVariables() declarations utils.R suppress R CMD check NSE notes

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: Framework: testthat edition 3 Location: tests/testthat/ Run : devtools::test() testthat::test_local() Test naming: test-<function_name>.R Environment: Set NOT_CRAN=true full test suite CI Skip helpers: tests/testthat/helper-skip.R provides skip_nhl_test(), skip_phf_test(), skip_pwhl_test() controlled env vars RUN_NHL_TESTS, RUN_PHF_TESTS, RUN_PWHL_TESTS (NHL/PWHL default true, PHF default false)

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: Key conventions: - skip_on_cran() tests hit live APIs - Test data.frame fastRhockey_data S3 classes - Validate row counts > 0 - Check expected column names - Use specific game IDs reproducible tests (e.g., 2023020001) - xG tests additionally require skip_if_not_installed("xgboost") - Deprecated function tests: wrap calls suppressWarnings()
code: test_that("NHL - Descriptive test name", {     skip_on_cran()     skip_nhl_test()     x <- nhl_function(param = value)     expect_s3_class(x, "data.frame")     expect_s3_class(x, "fastRhockey_data")     expect_true(nrow(x) > 0)     expect_true("expected_column" %in% names(x)) })

title: fastRhockey
text: fastRhockey R package accessing hockey data NHL (National Hockey League) PWHL (Professional Women’s Hockey League) via public web APIs. provides structured data frames play--play, schedule, standings, roster, draft, player stats, team data. package also includes integrated expected goals (xG) models trained via XGBoost. Part SportsDataverse family R/Python packages sports analytics.

title: fastRhockey
text: NHL Data – Game feeds, boxscores, play--play shifts, schedules, standings, rosters, draft picks, player/team stats, playoffs, broadcast information via api-web.nhle.com api.nhle.com/stats PWHL Data – Schedules, standings, play--play, player box scores, team rosters, stat leaders, player profiles, game logs, career stats, player search, league leaders, transactions, streaks, playoff brackets, game summaries, scorebar via HockeyTech API xG Models – Expected goals predictions NHL play--play data using XGBoost models (5v5, special teams, penalty shots) Full Season Loaders – load_nhl_pbp(), load_nhl_schedule(), load_nhl_team_box(), load_nhl_player_box(), load_nhl_rosters() bulk historical NHL data; load_pwhl_pbp(), load_pwhl_schedule(), load_pwhl_player_box(), load_pwhl_rosters() PWHL Note: PHF (Premier Hockey Federation) functions deprecated v1.0.0. league ceased operations; use PWHL functions instead.

what: Calculate expected goals (xG) for play-by-play data — helper_nhl_calculate_xg
title: Calculate expected goals (xG) for play-by-play data — helper_nhl_calculate_xg
text: Adds xg column play--play data frame using fastRhockey xG models (XGBoost). models trained NHL PBP data 2010-2024 using methodology hockeyR xG models. Three models used: 5v5 model — Even-strength situations Special teams model — Power play, short-handed, non-5v5 situations (adds skater count features) Penalty shot model — constant xG value penalty shot attempts Models loaded package namespace package load via .onLoad() (see zzz.R).

text: tibble model features plus identifiers (season, game_id, event_id, strength_state).

title: Prepare PBP data for xG model predictions — helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data
text: function: Filters shootouts, penalty shots, shift-change events. Computes lag features (last event type/team, time since last, distance last, event zones). Adds era dummies four training eras (2011-13, 2014-18, 2019-21, 2022+). Computes tactical booleans: rebound, rush, cross_ice_event. One-hot encodes shot_type last_event_type. Pads missing model features zero xgboost DMatrix dimensions match trained models.

title: NHL Club Schedule — nhl_club_schedule
text: Returns schedule data given team. Supports season, month, week views.

code: nhl_club_schedule(   team_abbr,   season = NULL,   month = NULL,   view = "season",   date = NULL )

title: NHL Club Schedule — nhl_club_schedule
text: team_abbr Three-letter team abbreviation (e.g., "TOR", "BOS") season Integer 4-digit end year season (e.g., 2026 2025-26 season), matching most_recent_nhl_season(). NULL, returns current season schedule. month Character month "YYYY-MM" format (e.g., "2025-01"). provided, returns month's schedule. Ignored view "week". view Character: "season" (default), "month", "week". date Character date "YYYY-MM-DD" format week view. NULL view="week", returns current week.

text: game_id (integer) NHL game ID, e.g. 2024020001.

title: NHL Game Boxscore — nhl_game_boxscore
text: named list containing: game_info — one-row tibble game metadata (id, season, game type, date, venue, teams, final score, shots goal). team_box — two-row tibble (away / home) team-level totals. skater_stats — tibble skater (forward + defense) individual stats teams. goalie_stats — tibble goalie individual stats teams.

title: NHL Game Play-by-Play — nhl_game_pbp
text: Returns detailed play--play data given NHL game, including event players, coordinates, -ice skaters, strength states, shot distance/angle, shift integration. Uses new NHL API api-web.nhle.com.

title: NHL Game Play-by-Play — nhl_game_pbp
code: nhl_game_pbp(game_id, include_shifts = TRUE, raw = FALSE)

title: NHL Game Play-by-Play — nhl_game_pbp
text: game_id Game unique ID (e.g. 2024020001) include_shifts Logical; whether integrate shift data -ice player tracking. Default TRUE. raw Logical; TRUE, return unprocessed API response list instead parsed data frame. Default FALSE.

title: NHL Game Play-by-Play — nhl_game_pbp
text: data frame one row per event (default), raw API response list raw = TRUE.

title: NHL Goalie Stats Leaders — nhl_goalie_stats_leaders
text: Returns league-wide goalie statistical leaders given season game type. Supports multiple stat categories.

title: NHL Goalie Stats Leaders — nhl_goalie_stats_leaders
code: nhl_goalie_stats_leaders(   season = NULL,   game_type = 2,   categories = NULL,   limit = NULL )

title: NHL Goalie Stats Leaders — nhl_goalie_stats_leaders
text: season Integer 4-digit year (e.g., 2024 2024-25 season). NULL, returns current season leaders. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs categories Character vector stat categories (e.g., "wins", "gaa", "savePctg", "shutouts"). NULL, returns available categories. limit Integer maximum number leaders per category. NULL, uses API default.

title: NHL Player Game Log — nhl_player_game_log
text: Returns game--game stats NHL player. Supports current season historical season lookups.

title: NHL Player Game Log — nhl_player_game_log
code: nhl_player_game_log(player_id, season = NULL, game_type = 2)

title: NHL Player Game Log — nhl_player_game_log
text: player_id Integer player ID (e.g., 8478402 Connor McDavid) season Integer 4-digit year (e.g., 2024 2024-25 season). NULL, returns current season game log. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs

title: NHL Player Game Log — nhl_player_game_log
text: Returns data frame game--game statistics.

title: NHL Player Game Log — nhl_player_game_log

title: NHL Player Info — nhl_player_info
text: Returns biographical career information NHL player. Uses NHL API (api-web.nhle.com).

code: nhl_player_info(player_id)

title: NHL Player Info — nhl_player_info
text: player_id Integer player ID (e.g., 8476899)

title: NHL Player Stats — nhl_player_stats
text: Returns season--season stats given player ID. Uses new NHL API player landing endpoint (api-web.nhle.com). Note: original statsapi.web.nhl.com endpoint retired. function now returns seasonTotals data player landing page, includes year--year stats across leagues (NHL, AHL, junior, etc.).

title: NHL Player Stats — nhl_player_stats
code: nhl_player_stats(player_id)

title: NHL Player Stats — nhl_player_stats
text: player_id Player unique ID (e.g., 8476899)

title: NHL Player Stats — nhl_player_stats
text: Returns data frame season--season stats player, including games played, goals, assists, league-specific columns.

title: NHL Roster Season — nhl_roster_season
text: Returns list seasons roster data available given team.

title: NHL Roster Season — nhl_roster_season
code: nhl_roster_season(team_abbr)

title: NHL Roster Season — nhl_roster_season
text: team_abbr Three-letter team abbreviation (e.g., "TOR", "BOS")

title: NHL Roster Season — nhl_roster_season
text: Returns data frame available roster seasons.

title: NHL Schedule — nhl_schedule
text: Returns NHL schedule data given day season. Uses NHL API (api-web.nhle.com).

title: NHL Schedule — nhl_schedule
code: nhl_schedule(day = NULL, season = NULL, team_abbr = NULL)

title: NHL Schedule — nhl_schedule
text: day Character date "YYYY-MM-DD" format. provided, returns games specific day. season Integer four-digit year end year season (e.g., 2026 2025-26 season), matching most_recent_nhl_season(). provided instead day, returns full season schedule. team_abbr Character three-letter team abbreviation (e.g., "TOR"). Required season used. NULL, loops teams.

title: NHL Schedule — nhl_schedule
text: Returns data frame game schedule information.

title: NHL Schedule Calendar — nhl_schedule_calendar
text: Returns schedule calendar showing dates games.

title: NHL Schedule Calendar — nhl_schedule_calendar
code: nhl_schedule_calendar(date = NULL)

title: NHL Schedule Calendar — nhl_schedule_calendar
text: date Character date "YYYY-MM-DD" format. NULL, returns current.

title: NHL Schedule Calendar — nhl_schedule_calendar
text: Returns list calendar data.

title: NHL Scores — nhl_scores
text: Returns scores games given date.

title: NHL Scores — nhl_scores
code: nhl_scores(date = NULL)

title: NHL Scores — nhl_scores
text: date Character date "YYYY-MM-DD" format. NULL, returns current scores.

title: NHL Scores — nhl_scores
text: Returns data frame game scores.

title: NHL Seasons — nhl_seasons
text: Returns list NHL seasons metadata.

title: NHL Seasons — nhl_seasons
code: nhl_seasons()

title: NHL Seasons — nhl_seasons
text: Returns data frame season information.

title: NHL Skater Stats Leaders — nhl_skater_stats_leaders
text: Returns league-wide skater statistical leaders given season game type. Supports multiple stat categories.

title: NHL Skater Stats Leaders — nhl_skater_stats_leaders
code: nhl_skater_stats_leaders(   season = NULL,   game_type = 2,   categories = NULL,   limit = NULL )

title: NHL Skater Stats Leaders — nhl_skater_stats_leaders
text: season Integer 4-digit year (e.g., 2024 2024-25 season). NULL, returns current season leaders. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs categories Character vector stat categories (e.g., "goals", "assists", "points", "plusMinus", "penaltyMins"). NULL, returns available categories. limit Integer maximum number leaders per category. NULL, uses API default.

title: NHL Skater Stats Leaders — nhl_skater_stats_leaders
text: Returns data frame skater leaders.

title: NHL Standings — nhl_standings
text: Returns current historical NHL standings. Uses new NHL API (api-web.nhle.com).

title: NHL Standings — nhl_standings
code: nhl_standings(date = NULL)

title: NHL Standings — nhl_standings
text: date Character date "YYYY-MM-DD" format. NULL, returns current standings.

title: NHL Stats API — Draft Summaries — nhl_stats_draft
text: draft_year Integer draft year (e.g., 2024). NULL, returns draft years (1963-present). limit Integer maximum number results. Default 100. start Integer start index pagination. Default 0. lang Character language code. Default "en".

text: Returns data frame columns: id, draft_year, rounds.

title: NHL Stats API — Draft Summaries — nhl_stats_draft

title: NHL Stats API — Goalie Stats — nhl_stats_goalies
text: report_type Character report type. Default "summary". Common types: "summary", "bios", "advanced", "daysrest", "penaltyShots", "savesByStrength", "shootout", "startedVsRelieved" season Character season "YYYYYYYY" format (e.g., "20242025"). NULL, uses current season. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs limit Integer maximum number results. Default 50. start Integer start index pagination. Default 0. sort Character sort column. Default varies report. direction Character sort direction: "DESC" "ASC". Default "DESC". lang Character language code. Default "en".

title: NHL Teams Stats — nhl_teams_stats
text: Returns NHL team player-level stats (skaters goalies) given team abbreviation season. Uses new NHL API club-stats endpoint (api-web.nhle.com). Breaking change: old team_id (integer) parameter replaced team_abbr (3-letter string). season parameter now accepts 4-digit year (e.g., 2024 2024-25 season).

title: NHL Teams Stats — nhl_teams_stats

title: PWHL Player Game Log — pwhl_player_game_log
text: Retrieves game--game statistics PWHL player given season.

title: PWHL Player Game Log — pwhl_player_game_log
code: pwhl_player_game_log(   player_id,   season = most_recent_pwhl_season(),   game_type = "regular" )

title: PWHL Player Game Log — pwhl_player_game_log
text: player_id Numeric player ID season Season (YYYY) pull game log , concluding year XXXX-YY format. Defaults most_recent_pwhl_season(). game_type Game type: "regular" (default), "preseason", "playoffs".

title: PWHL Player Game Log — pwhl_player_game_log

title: Update or create a fastRhockey NHL play-by-play database — update_nhl_db
text: dbdir Directory database shall located dbname File name existing desired SQLite database within dbdir tblname name play play data table within database force_rebuild Hybrid parameter (logical numeric) rebuild parts complete play play data table within database (please see details information) db_connection DBIConnection object, returned DBI::dbConnect() (please see details information)

title: Update or create a fastRhockey NHL play-by-play database — update_nhl_db
text: Logical TRUE/FALSE

title: Update or create a fastRhockey NHL play-by-play database — update_nhl_db
text: function creates updates data table name tblname within SQLite database (drivers via db_connection) located dbdir named dbname. data table combines play play data every available game back 2010 season adds recent completed games soon available fastRhockey. argument force_rebuild hybrid type. can rebuild play play data table either whole fastRhockey era (force_rebuild = TRUE) just specified seasons (e.g. force_rebuild = c(2019, 2020)). Please note following behavior: force_rebuild = TRUE: data table name tblname removed completely rebuilt scratch. helpful new columns added -Season. force_rebuild = c(2019, 2020): data table name tblname preserved rows 2019 2020 seasons deleted re-added. intended used ongoing seasons ESPN's data provider can make changes underlying data week. parameter db_connection intended advanced users want use DBI drivers, MariaDB, Postgres odbc. Please note arguments dbdir dbname dropped case db_connection provided argument tblname still used write data table database.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: Version 1.0.0 signals API stability NHL PWHL endpoints. PHF functions formally deprecated via lifecycle. Premier Hockey Federation ceased operations; use PWHL functions instead. Functions removed future release. Consolidated new NHL API functions existing NHL function names rather creating _v2 variants, since original API endpoints deprecated NHL. means nhl_game_feed(), nhl_game_boxscore(), nhl_schedule(), nhl_teams(), nhl_teams_roster(), nhl_player_info() now use new api-web.nhle.com endpoints directly.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: pwhl_pbp() – PWHL play--play data. pwhl_player_box() – PWHL player box scores (skaters goalies). pwhl_game_info() – PWHL game information metadata. pwhl_game_summary() – Detailed game summary (scoring, penalties, shots period, three stars). pwhl_standings() – PWHL league standings. pwhl_stats() – PWHL stat leaders (skaters goalies). pwhl_leaders() – League leaders (top scorers top goalies). pwhl_season_id() – PWHL season ID lookup (now API-driven fallback). pwhl_player_info() – Player biographical profile information. pwhl_player_game_log() – Per-game statistics player season. pwhl_player_stats() – Career season--season statistics player. pwhl_player_search() – Search players name. pwhl_transactions() – Player transactions season. pwhl_streaks() – Player streak data season. pwhl_playoff_bracket() – Playoff bracket / series data. pwhl_scorebar() – Recent upcoming game scores. most_recent_pwhl_season() – Utility get current PWHL season year. load_pwhl_pbp() – Load pre-scraped PWHL play--play data. load_pwhl_player_box() – Load pre-scraped PWHL player box scores. load_pwhl_schedule() – Load pre-scraped PWHL schedules. load_pwhl_rosters() – Load pre-scraped PWHL team rosters. update_pwhl_db() – Update create PWHL play--play database.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: nhl_where_to_watch() – streaming/broadcast availability. nhl_partner_game_odds() – partner game odds country. NHL API migration api-web.nhle.com: nhl_game_feed(), nhl_game_boxscore(), nhl_schedule(), nhl_teams(), nhl_teams_roster(), nhl_player_info(), nhl_player_game_log(), nhl_stats_goalies(), nhl_stats_skaters(), nhl_stats_teams(), nhl_stats_misc(), nhl_gamecenter_landing(), nhl_gamecenter_right_rail(), nhl_scoreboard(), nhl_scores(), nhl_seasons(), nhl_tv_schedule(), nhl_game_story(), nhl_game_content(), nhl_game_shifts(), nhl_playoff_bracket(), nhl_playoff_schedule(), nhl_playoff_carousel(). xG model integration via helper_nhl_calculate_xg().

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: Fixed nhl_draft_year() – NHL API removed /v1/draft/picks/{year} endpoint. function now iterates rounds 1-7 using /v1/draft/picks/{year}/{round}. Fixed pwhl_stats() – resolved “object ‘players’ found” error API calls failed. Fixed team ID resolution skater stats. Fixed pwhl_schedule() – season column inadvertently dropped output. now included. Fixed refresh_xg_models() – resolved “change value locked binding” error storing xG models package environment (.xg_env) instead top-level bindings. Fixed NAMESPACE: removed import(tidyverse) violated CRAN policy. Individual packages (dplyr, tidyr, etc.) already imported.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: PHF functions: phf_schedule(), phf_standings(), phf_pbp(), phf_player_box(), phf_team_box(), phf_team_roster(), phf_team_stats(), phf_player_stats(), phf_leaders(), phf_league_info(), phf_game_all(), phf_game_raw(), phf_game_details(), phf_game_summary(). PHF loaders: load_phf_pbp(), load_phf_team_box(), load_phf_player_box(), load_phf_schedule(), load_phf_rosters(), update_phf_db(). Utility: most_recent_phf_season().

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: pwhl_season_id() now retrieves season data dynamically HockeyTech API instead using hardcoded lookup table. Falls back hardcoded data API unavailable. Added internal helpers .pwhl_api(), .pwhl_modulekit_url(), .pwhl_gc_url(), .pwhl_resolve_season_id() reduce JSONP parsing boilerplate across PWHL functions. Added lifecycle package formal deprecation management. Updated testthat dependency >= 3.0.0. Complete test coverage 95 exported functions (482 tests). Added environment-controlled test toggles (RUN_NHL_TESTS, RUN_PHF_TESTS, RUN_PWHL_TESTS) via tests/testthat/helper-skip.R. CI workflow environment variables now match helper-skip.R names. Updated test expectations match current API responses. nhl_where_to_watch() returns NULL gracefully NHL /v1/--watch endpoint unavailable. Updated _pkgdown.yml reorganized reference sections deprecated function categories.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: Updated CONTRIBUTING.md naming conventions, testing environment variables, conventional commits guide, deprecation process. Updated PR issue templates. Updated CLAUDE.md .github/copilot-instructions.md reflect v1.0.0 changes new PWHL endpoints. Added data-raw/pr_devel.md development scratchpad. Added cran_comments.md CRAN submission. Updated _pkgdown.yml reorganized PWHL reference sections. Added R CMD check CI workflow pkgdown deployment workflow.
